content: The intrinsic curiosity bonus signals used in ICM/RND curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning metric for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems, where unexpected prediction errors that drive curiosity also flag which episodic memory traces are high-value for future active exploration. This creates a self-tuning memory system that naturally prioritizes storing the uncertain, learnable experiences that align with the agent’s active learning goals, rather than relying on hand-engineered retention rules.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
